projects.role: Provider, Deployer, Product manufacturer, Distributor, Importer

allComplianceTasks.description: Establish and document continuous risk management system throughout AI system lifecycle
Implement data governance practices for training, validation, and testing datasets
Prepare technical documentation before placing AI system on market
Maintain record-keeping and automatic recording of events (logs)
Keep automatically generated logs secure and immutable
Draw up instructions for use in appropriate digital format or otherwise
Design system to enable effective oversight by natural persons
Design to achieve appropriate level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
Put quality management system in place ensuring compliance with EU AI Act
Non-EU providers must appoint authorized representative by written mandate
Ensure imported high-risk AI system complies with EU AI Act requirements
Verify AI system bears CE marking and is accompanied by required documentation
Take appropriate technical and organisational measures to use high-risk AI systems in accordance with instructions
Carry out fundamental rights impact assessment before deploying high-risk AI system
Draw up EU declaration of conformity and keep it at disposal of national authorities
Affix CE marking to high-risk AI system or on packaging/accompanying documentation
Register use of high-risk AI system in EU database for public authorities and bodies
Register high-risk AI system in EU database before placing on market
Establish and document post-market monitoring system proportionate to nature of AI technologies
Report serious incidents to market surveillance authorities within specified timeframes
Upon request, provide market surveillance authorities with all documentation and information necessary to investigate serious incident
Ensure transparency in AI systems that interact with natural persons, detect emotions, generate/manipulate content

assessmentTools.name: Prompt Injection Detector
assessmentTools.description: Detects and prevents prompt injection attacks in AI systems. Analyzes input prompts to identify potential security vulnerabilities including malicious instructions, data exfiltration attempts, and unauthorized command execution.

assessmentTools.name: Security Frameworks
assessmentTools.description: Implements comprehensive security frameworks and best practices for AI systems. Provides structured approach to cybersecurity including threat modeling, vulnerability assessment, and security controls implementation.

assessmentTools.name: Grype Vulnerability Scanner
assessmentTools.description: Scans container images and filesystems for known vulnerabilities in dependencies and libraries. Identifies security risks in AI system components.

assessmentTools.name: Safety (PyUp)
assessmentTools.description: Checks Python dependencies for known security vulnerabilities and suggests updates. Maintains secure software supply chain for Python-based AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: Snyk
assessmentTools.description: Developer-first security platform that finds and automatically fixes vulnerabilities in code, dependencies, containers, and infrastructure as code.

assessmentTools.name: OSS Scorecard
assessmentTools.description: Assesses security posture of open-source projects using automated checks. Evaluates security practices of dependencies used in AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA)
assessmentTools.description: Conducts comprehensive assessment of AI system impact on fundamental rights including privacy, non-discrimination, and human dignity.

assessmentTools.name: Data Classification
assessmentTools.description: Automatically classifies and labels sensitive data including personal data, special categories of data, and confidential information.

assessmentTools.name: GDPR Compliance
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive toolkit for ensuring General Data Protection Regulation compliance in AI systems. Validates data processing activities, consent mechanisms, data subject rights implementation, and privacy-by-design principles.

assessmentTools.name: HIPAA Compliance
assessmentTools.description: Ensures Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act compliance for AI systems processing protected health information.

assessmentTools.name: PCI DSS Compliance
assessmentTools.description: Validates Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard compliance for AI systems handling payment card data.

assessmentTools.name: Detoxify
assessmentTools.description: Machine learning model that detects toxic comments and harmful content in text. Identifies toxicity, severe toxicity, obscenity, threats, insults, and identity-based hate.

assessmentTools.name: Hate Speech Detector
assessmentTools.description: Identifies hate speech and discriminatory language in AI system outputs. Detects offensive content targeting protected characteristics.

assessmentTools.name: ClaimBuster API
assessmentTools.description: Identifies check-worthy factual claims in text for fact-checking purposes. Helps ensure AI systems generate verifiable and accurate information.

assessmentTools.name: Fact Checker
assessmentTools.description: Verifies factual accuracy of AI-generated content against reliable sources. Reduces misinformation and ensures AI systems produce truthful outputs.

assessmentTools.name: AI Content Detector
assessmentTools.description: Identifies AI-generated content to ensure transparency in synthetic content.

assessmentTools.name: Perspective API
assessmentTools.description: Analyzes text for various attributes including toxicity, profanity, identity attacks, and threats using machine learning.

assessmentTools.name: TextBlob Sentiment Analysis
assessmentTools.description: Analyzes sentiment and subjectivity in text outputs to ensure balanced and appropriate communication.

assessmentTools.name: VADER Sentiment Analysis
assessmentTools.description: Valence Aware Dictionary and sEntiment Reasoner specifically attuned to social media text sentiment.

assessmentTools.name: AI Fairness 360 (AIF360)
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive toolkit from IBM to examine, report, and mitigate discrimination and bias in machine learning models.

assessmentTools.name: Fairlearn
assessmentTools.description: Python package for assessing and improving fairness of machine learning models.

assessmentTools.name: Aequitas
assessmentTools.description: Bias and fairness audit toolkit for machine learning models.

assessmentTools.name: Bias Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Systematic evaluation framework for identifying and quantifying algorithmic bias in AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: Disaggregated Evaluation
assessmentTools.description: Evaluates model performance separately across different demographic subgroups to identify performance disparities.

assessmentTools.name: Perspective API
assessmentTools.description: Uses machine learning to identify potential toxic, threatening, or unwelcome comments.

assessmentTools.name: Content Moderation API
assessmentTools.description: Automated content moderation system that detects inappropriate, offensive, or biased content in AI-generated outputs.

assessmentTools.name: Explainability Planning
assessmentTools.description: Strategic framework for designing explainable AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations)
assessmentTools.description: Unified approach to explain machine learning model predictions using game-theoretic Shapley values.

assessmentTools.name: LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations)
assessmentTools.description: Explains predictions of any machine learning classifier by approximating it locally with an interpretable model.

assessmentTools.name: Captum
assessmentTools.description: Model interpretability library for PyTorch providing attribution algorithms for neural networks.

assessmentTools.name: InterpretML
assessmentTools.description: Open-source package for training interpretable models and explaining black-box systems.

assessmentTools.name: What-If Tool
assessmentTools.description: Interactive visual tool for probing machine learning models.

assessmentTools.name: AI Transparency Labels
assessmentTools.description: Generates standardized transparency labels for AI systems similar to nutrition labels.

assessmentTools.name: Model Card Generation
assessmentTools.description: Creates comprehensive documentation of machine learning models including intended use, performance characteristics, limitations, and ethical considerations.

assessmentTools.name: AI Safety
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive AI safety framework ensuring systems behave as intended and avoid harm.

assessmentTools.name: AI Safety Planning
assessmentTools.description: Plans AI safety measures including alignment, guardrails, red teaming, and regulatory compliance.

assessmentTools.name: Red Team Testing
assessmentTools.description: Adversarial testing methodology to identify vulnerabilities and safety issues in AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: Guardrails Implementation
assessmentTools.description: Implements input validation and output filtering guardrails for AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: CodeCarbon
assessmentTools.description: Tracks and estimates CO2 emissions produced by computing resources during AI model training and inference.

assessmentTools.name: Cloud Carbon Footprint
assessmentTools.description: Measures, monitors, and reduces cloud computing carbon emissions across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

assessmentTools.name: ML CO2 Impact
assessmentTools.description: Calculates CO2 emissions from machine learning model training based on hardware, runtime, and regional energy sources.

assessmentTools.name: WattTime Carbon
assessmentTools.description: Provides real-time carbon intensity data for electricity grids to optimize AI workload scheduling.

assessmentTools.name: Evidently AI
assessmentTools.description: Open-source ML model monitoring and testing framework for evaluating data quality, data drift, and model performance.

assessmentTools.name: Alibi Detect
assessmentTools.description: Outlier detection, adversarial detection, and data drift monitoring library for ML systems.

assessmentTools.name: RAGAS (Retrieval Augmented Generation Assessment)
assessmentTools.description: Evaluation framework for RAG systems measuring faithfulness, answer relevance, and context precision.

assessmentTools.name: DeepEval
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive LLM testing and evaluation framework measuring factual accuracy, completeness, and consistency.

assessmentTools.name: HuggingFace Evaluate
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive library of evaluation metrics for NLP, computer vision, and multimodal models.

assessmentTools.name: LangSmith
assessmentTools.description: LLM observability and evaluation platform for debugging, testing, and monitoring language model applications.

assessmentTools.name: Weights & Biases (W&B)
assessmentTools.description: ML experiment tracking, model versioning, and performance monitoring platform.

assessmentTools.name: Promptfoo
assessmentTools.description: LLM testing and evaluation tool for prompt engineering and model comparison.

assessmentTools.name: Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Design
assessmentTools.description: Framework for designing and implementing human-in-the-loop systems ensuring meaningful human oversight of AI decisions.

assessmentTools.name: Incident Responder
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive incident management system for high-risk AI systems per Article 73.

assessmentTools.name: Conformance Calibration
assessmentTools.description: Systematic change management system ensuring conformity assessment procedures.

assessmentTools.name: Risk Management
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive risk management framework for identifying, assessing, and mitigating risks throughout the AI system lifecycle.

assessmentTools.name: Toxicity Detection Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Automated assessment tool for detecting toxic, harmful, or offensive content in AI system outputs.

assessmentTools.name: Hate Speech Detection Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Specialized detection system for identifying hate speech, discriminatory language, and targeted harassment.

assessmentTools.name: AI Hallucination Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Detects and assesses AI hallucinations, factual inaccuracies, and false information generation.

assessmentTools.name: AI Ethics Fact-Checking System Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive fact-checking and verification system for AI outputs ensuring ethical accuracy and truthfulness.

assessmentTools.name: Content Toxicity Analysis Assessment
assessmentTools.description: In-depth analysis tool for evaluating content toxicity levels across multiple dimensions.

assessmentTools.name: AI Alignment Framework Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Ensures AI system alignment with organizational values, ethical principles, and regulatory requirements.

assessmentTools.name: Claim Verification Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Automated verification system for validating factual claims in AI-generated content.

assessmentTools.name: AI Generated Content Detection Assessment
assessmentTools.description: Detects and labels AI-generated content including text, images, audio, and video.

assessmentTools.name: AI Governance
assessmentTools.description: Establishes governance structures and processes for responsible AI development and deployment.

assessmentTools.name: AI Ethics Framework
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive framework for implementing ethical AI principles including fairness, transparency, accountability, and human oversight.

assessmentTools.name: AI Ethics Advisor
assessmentTools.description: Advisory tool providing guidance on ethical AI development and deployment decisions.

assessmentTools.name: Ethics Review Board
assessmentTools.description: Structured ethics review process for evaluating AI systems before deployment.

assessmentTools.name: Validating AI Ethics and Fairness
assessmentTools.description: Comprehensive validation toolkit for assessing AI system ethics and fairness.

assessmentTools.name: AI Logging System
assessmentTools.description: Automated logging system for AI operations capturing decisions, inputs, outputs, and system events.

assessmentTools.name: AI System Registry
assessmentTools.description: Central registry for documenting and tracking AI systems throughout their lifecycle.

assessmentTools.name: Quality Management System Tracker
assessmentTools.description: Tracks quality management system implementation and maintenance for AI systems.

assessmentTools.name: CE Marking Generator
assessmentTools.description: Generates CE marking documentation for high-risk AI systems entering the EU market.

prohibitedPractices.label: Subliminal / manipulative / deceptive techniques that materially distort behaviour and are likely to cause significant harm
Exploitation of vulnerabilities (age, disability, or social/economic situation) to distort behaviour likely causing significant harm
Social scoring leading to detrimental/unfavourable treatment (esp. unjustified/disproportionate)
Criminal offence risk assessment / prediction based solely on profiling or personality traits (individual predictive policing)
prohibitedPractices.exceptionCondition: AI system is used to support a human assessment based on objective and verifiable facts directly linked to criminal activity (not solely profiling). (Art.5(1)(d))

prohibitedPractices.label: Untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV to build / expand facial recognition databases
Emotion recognition in the workplace or in education settings
prohibitedPractices.exceptionCondition: AI system is for medical or safety reasons. (Art.5(1)(f))

prohibitedPractices.label: Biometric categorisation that infers or predicts sensitive traits (e.g., race, political opinions, religion, trade union membership, sexual orientation)
prohibitedPractices.exceptionCondition: AI system is for labelling or filtering of lawfully acquired biometric datasets, such as images, based on biometric data or categorizing of biometric data in the area of law enforcement. (Art.5(1)(g))

prohibitedPractices.label: Real-time remote biometric identification (RBI) in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
prohibitedPractices.exceptionCondition: Only if strictly necessary for one of the listed objectives (victims / imminent serious threat / serious crime suspect) and with safeguards + authorisation requirements (Art. 5(2)–(7)).

highRiskCategories.category: Biometric identification and categorisation of natural persons
highRiskCategories.description: Remote biometric identification systems and biometric categorisation systems

highRiskCategories.category: Management and operation of critical infrastructure
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used as safety components in the management and operation of road traffic and supply of water, gas, heating and electricity
AI systems intended to be used for determining access to educational institutions, evaluating learning outcomes, or assessing appropriate level of education

highRiskCategories.category: Employment, workers management and access to self-employment
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used for recruitment, making decisions affecting employment, task allocation, and monitoring/evaluation of persons in work-related contractual relationships

highRiskCategories.category: Access to and enjoyment of essential private services and public services and benefits
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to evaluate creditworthiness, assess eligibility for public assistance, or dispatch emergency services
AI systems intended for individual risk assessment, lie detection, emotion recognition, deep fake detection, or evaluating evidence reliability

highRiskCategories.category: Migration, asylum and border control management
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended for assessing risk posed by persons, examining applications, or detecting irregular migrants

highRiskCategories.category: Administration of justice and democratic processes
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to assist judicial authorities in researching and interpreting facts and law
